content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me locate atom C:17 and identify its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see atom C:17 (carbon, index 17) in the center-right portion of the molecule. Looking at the bonds directly connected to C:17, I can identify:

- C:16 (carbon, index 16) - connected to the left
- C:18 (carbon, index 18) - connected to the right
- C:23 (carbon, index 23) - connected upward

I can confirm these are covalent bonds by seeing the lines connecting these atoms to C:17. There are no hydrogen atoms directly bonded to C:17 in this structure - the hydrogens visible (H:20, H:21, H:32, H:34, etc.) are bonded to other carbon atoms.

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 9986, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_9986.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 17 (C) are:
- Atom index 16, element C
- Atom index 18, element C  
- Atom index 23, element C

Geomconf_index: 9986

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 17, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
